perturbation.original_text: 6. This Agreement will be effective as of the date of this Agreement, but will apply to any Confidential Information disclosed to the Receiving Party from the Disclosing Party prior to such date and will continue in perpetuity.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. This Agreement will be effective as of the date of this Agreement and will terminate 30 days after the signing of a SAP Service agreement between the parties. Any data exchanged or provided after the termination date are not covered by this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states the agreement is perpetual. This change makes the agreement terminate after 30 days of signing a SAP service agreement. This creates a contradiction with Section 9, because section 9 states the agreement terminates when a SAP Service agreement is terminated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. This Agreement will terminate on the date and time the Agreement between the Parties over the provision of SAP Services is terminated. In the event this Agreement is terminated, and the Disclosing Party so requests, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy (and certify destruction of) all Confidential Information which it received from the Disclosing Party along with all copies. The obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement or the return of the Confidential Information to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement will continue in perpetuity, while the changed text makes the agreement terminate 30 days after a SAP service agreement is signed. Section 9 states the agreement terminates when the SAP Service agreement is terminated. The changed text creates a direct conflict with Section 9 because the termination conditions are different.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Title to, interest in, and all other rights of ownership to Confidential Information shall remain with the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Title to, interest in, and all other rights of ownership to Confidential Information shall transfer to the Receiving Party upon successful completion of the SAP service implementation.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction because the original text states that the ownership remains with the disclosing party. The change indicates ownership transfers to the receiving party when the SAP service implementation is complete. This is in direct conflict with the purpose of a Non-Disclosure agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. The confidential, proprietary and trade secret information of the Disclosing Party (hereinafter "Confidential Information") provided hereunder is any and all information, in whatever form (including electronic data) that will be disclosed to facilitate the potential transaction of business (related to the provision of SAP Services to HRM) between the Parties, including, but not limited to, portions or parts of the November 1, 2012 Agreement (including any schedules or appendices) between the Province of Nova Scotia and IBM, each Party's disclosure of intellectual property, techniques, sketches, drawings, models, inventions, know-how, processes, apparatus, equipment, algorithms, software programs, software source documents, and formulae related to the current, future and proposed products, documents and services, research, experimental work, development, design details and specifications, engineering, financial information, procurement requirements, purchasing, manufacturing, licensing, sales or service customer lists, business forecasts, sales and merchandizing, and marketing plans and information. For purposes of this Agreement confidential, proprietary and trade secret information of the Province’s SAP Service Provi der or any other Province’s Service Provider, any parent company, subsidiary or affiliate of the Disclosing party shall be considered Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party for purposes of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 8 clearly states that ownership of Confidential Information remains with the Disclosing Party. The changed text in section 8 directly contradicts this by stating that ownership transfers to the Receiving Party upon completion of the SAP service implementation. This creates a strong and direct conflict. This directly breaks compatibility with the contradicted text in section 1, which defines Confidential Information as belonging to the Disclosing Party, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as outlined in the explanation given.

perturbation.original_text: 2. The term Confidential Information shall not include information, which is: (a) in the public domain other than by a breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; or 2 (b) rightfully received from a third party with no duty of confidentiality; or (c) rightfully known to the Receiving Party without any limitation on use or disclosure prior to its receipt from the Disclosing Party; or (d) independently developed by employees, agents or consultants of the Receiving Party; or (e) generally made available to third parties by the Disclosing Party without restriction on disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. The term 'Confidential Information' shall not include information, which is: (a) in the public domain other than by a breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; or 2 (b) rightfully received from a third party with no duty of confidentiality; or (c) rightfully known to the Receiving Party without any limitation on use or disclosure prior to its receipt from the Disclosing Party; or (d) independently developed by employees, agents or consultants of the Receiving Party; or (e) generally made available to third parties by the Disclosing Party without restriction on disclosure; or (f) ANY INFORMATION RELATED TO BUDGETARY ALLOCATIONS OR FINANCIAL PROJECTIONS, regardless of its source or prior classification.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds an exception to what constitutes 'Confidential Information', by excluding any data related to budgetary allocations or financial projections. This contradicts the broad definition in section 1, where 'financial information' is explicitly listed as an example of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. The confidential, proprietary and trade secret information of the Disclosing Party (hereinafter "Confidential Information") provided hereunder is any and all information, in whatever form (including electronic data) that will be disclosed to facilitate the potential transaction of business (related to the provision of SAP Services to HRM) between the Parties, including, but not limited to, portions or parts of the November 1, 2012 Agreement (including any schedules or appendices) between the Province of Nova Scotia and IBM, each Party's disclosure of intellectual property, techniques, sketches, drawings, models, inventions, know-how, processes, apparatus, equipment, algorithms, software programs, software source documents, and formulae related to the current, future and proposed products, documents and services, research, experimental work, development, design details and specifications, engineering, financial information, procurement requirements, purchasing, manufacturing, licensing, sales or service customer lists, business forecasts, sales and merchandizing, and marketing plans and information. For purposes of this Agreement confidential, proprietary and trade secret information of the Province’s SAP Service Provi der or any other Province’s Service Provider, any parent company, subsidiary or affiliate of the Disclosing party shall be considered Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party for purposes of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds section (f) to the definition of 'Confidential Information', excluding 'ANY INFORMATION RELATED TO BUDGETARY ALLOCATIONS OR FINANCIAL PROJECTIONS'. This directly contradicts the definition of 'Confidential Information' in Section 1 (the contradicted text), which explicitly includes 'financial information' as an example of Confidential Information. Therefore, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction, as the two sections now define 'Confidential Information' in mutually exclusive ways.

perturbation.original_text: 3. The Receiving Party agrees that Confidential Information shall be used only for the purposes of facilitating the business relationship between the Parties. Additionally, subject to section 4 below, the Receiving Party shall not disclose Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall maintain the Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care that the Receiving Party uses to protect its own similar categories of confidential and proprietary information, but no less than a reasonable degree of care under the circumstances. The Receiving Party shall not make any copies of Confidential Information except as necessary to perform hereunder. Each Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by such Party, its employees, agents, officials, representatives or consultants acting within the scope of their engagement or employment.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. The Receiving Party agrees that Confidential Information shall be used only for the purposes of facilitating the business relationship between the Parties UNLESS THE INFORMATION IS DEEMED NECESSARY FOR PUBLIC DISCLOSURE PURSUANT TO A VALID LEGAL REQUEST. Additionally, subject to section 4 below, the Receiving Party shall not disclose Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party, EXCEPT WHERE REQUIRED BY LAW. The Receiving Party shall maintain the Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care that the Receiving Party uses to protect its own similar categories of confidential and proprietary information, but no less than a reasonable degree of care under the circumstances. The Receiving Party shall not make any copies of Confidential Information except as necessary to perform hereunder. Each Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by such Party, its employees, agents, officials, representatives or consultants acting within the scope of their engagement or employment.
perturbation.explanation: By introducing the phrases 'UNLESS THE INFORMATION IS DEEMED NECESSARY FOR PUBLIC DISCLOSURE PURSUANT TO A VALID LEGAL REQUEST' and 'EXCEPT WHERE REQUIRED BY LAW', this change creates ambiguity about the scope of confidentiality. While section 10 mentions the Freedom of Information Act, this modification introduces an additional layer of uncertainty about what other legal requirements might override the confidentiality agreement. It also introduces a conflict with what the agreement is being signed for, if there is a way to be made public.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. The Parties acknowledge and understand that all documentation and information , including Confidential Information, provided to the Province of Nova Scotia or HRM, as the case may be, pursuant to this Agreement shall be kept confidential, subject to the provisions of the Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act , Province of Nova Scotia, and such other laws of the Province of Nova Scotia and Canada which may apply from time to time.
perturbation.justification: The original text of section 3 states that Confidential Information shall only be used for facilitating the business relationship between the Parties, and that the Receiving Party shall not disclose Confidential Information to any third party without prior written approval. The changed text introduces exceptions: 'UNLESS THE INFORMATION IS DEEMED NECESSARY FOR PUBLIC DISCLOSURE PURSUANT TO A VALID LEGAL REQUEST' and 'EXCEPT WHERE REQUIRED BY LAW'. This directly contradicts the confidentiality previously agreed upon, creating ambiguity about when and how information can be disclosed. Section 10 acknowledges confidentiality but also refers to the Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act. The additions to section 3 create a strong contradiction because they expand the potential legal requirements that override the confidentiality agreement beyond the FOIPOP Act, introducing uncertainty that makes sections 3 and 10 incompatible.

perturbation.original_text: 9. This Agreement will terminate on the date and time the Agreement between the Parties over the provision of SAP Services is terminated. In the event this Agreement is terminated, and the Disclosing Party so requests, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy (and certify destruction of) all Confidential Information which it received from the Disclosing Party along with all copies. The obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement or the return of the Confidential Information to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. This Agreement will terminate on the date and time the Agreement between the Parties over the provision of SAP Services is terminated. In the event this Agreement is terminated, and the Disclosing Party so requests, the Receiving Party shall retain all Confidential Information indefinitely for archival purposes. The obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement shall not survive the termination of this Agreement or the return of the Confidential Information to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: This edit introduces a contradiction concerning what the Receiving Party should do with confidential information upon termination of the agreement. Originally, the Receiving Party was required to return or destroy the information; now, the Receiving Party must retain it indefinitely. This is a direct conflict that could lead to legal confusion about compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. This Agreement will terminate on the date and time the Agreement between the Parties over the provision of SAP Services is terminated. In the event this Agreement is terminated, and the Disclosing Party so requests, the Receiving Party shall retain all Confidential Information indefinitely for archival purposes. The obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement shall not survive the termination of this Agreement or the return of the Confidential Information to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 9 introduces a direct contradiction. The original text stated that upon termination, the Receiving Party should return or destroy confidential information. The changed text states that the Receiving Party shall retain all Confidential Information indefinitely for archival purposes. This directly contradicts the original instruction and the contradicted text, creating an in-text contradiction as defined by 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.
